title: Gated synthetic-voice adapter (clearly-licensed, talent-consented, labeled)
context: Synthetic narration is a gated, opt-in path: it may be used only with a TTS voice whose underlying talent has consented to synthesis and redistribution, and all output must be labeled AI-synthesized. This adapter wraps the chosen synthetic-voice tool in adapters/tts/ behind a neutral interface, enforces the consent gate, records voice provenance, and ensures the AI-synthesized label is applied. It must never produce an imitation of an identifiable real person. Only enabled when the synthetic path is confirmed in scope.
objective: Implement the gated synthetic-voice adapter in adapters/tts/ that wraps a clearly-licensed TTS voice, enforces the consent and provenance gate, and ensures all output is labeled AI-synthesized.
acceptanceCriteria: Wraps a TTS voice model that is clearly licensed for redistribution of generated audio and whose underlying voice talent has documented consent to synthesis and redistribution.
Enforces the consent gate: refuses to render without a valid synthetic-provenance record (model, version, voice id, license, talent-consent attestation).
Applies the AI-synthesized label to all output audio and records the full synthetic-provenance record in the per-work data-001 record.
Explicitly refuses to render a voice described as an imitation or clone of an identifiable real person; emits a clear refusal reason.
Passes pnpm build and pnpm test (unit tests covering: consent-gate enforcement, label application, provenance record structure, refusal of identifiable-person imitation); pnpm lint clean.
Licensed MIT; DCO sign-off on all commits.
resources: PLAN.md (synthetic narration gated path, adapters/tts/ component, synthetic-voice scope)
output: A merged PR adding adapters/tts/ with the synthetic-voice adapter implementation, unit tests, and documentation covering the supported voice model, consent-gate enforcement, labeling, provenance record fields, and the identifiable-person refusal rule.
